Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Deep Learning Training
Short Hypothesis: A curvature-aware precision routing framework can significantly improve the stability and efficiency of deep learning training by dynamically adjusting the precision of gradients and parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape.
Related Work: Previous work has explored the use of mixed precision training and curvature information to improve training efficiency and stability. However, these approaches often treat precision adjustments as static or heuristic-based. This proposal introduces a dynamic, curvature-aware precision routing framework that adapts precision levels in real-time, ensuring optimal resource utilization and numerical stability.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework for deep learning training that dynamically adjusts the precision of gradients and parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape. By leveraging spectral quantization and block-level precision tiering, our approach ensures numerical stability and computational efficiency. We formalize the routing protocol using deterministic spectral estimators and pre-execution diagnostic loops, which isolate algorithmic pathologies from infrastructural noise. The framework is validated through empirical experiments on standard benchmarks, demonstrating significant improvements in training time and memory usage while maintaining or improving model accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the effectiveness of curvature-aware precision routing on a variety of deep learning models, including ResNet-18, EfficientNet-B0, and Transformer models.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, accuracy, and stability metrics such as gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning.

Experiments.Description: Compare the proposed framework against static mixed-precision training and full-precision training on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, and model accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Conduct ablation studies to analyze the impact of different components of the curvature-aware precision routing framework, such as spectral quantization and block-level precision tiering.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, and model accuracy.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the robustness of the framework under varying conditions, including different batch sizes, learning rates, and hardware configurations.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, and model accuracy.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The computational overhead of curvature estimation and precision adjustment may introduce additional latency, which could offset the gains in training efficiency.
The framework's effectiveness may vary across different model architectures and datasets, requiring further tuning and validation.
The dynamic nature of precision adjustments may introduce additional complexity in the training pipeline, potentially leading to implementation challenges.
